evals.prompt: Generate a changelog for version 2.5.0. Commits since v2.4.0 include: 3 feat commits (notifications, export CSV, dark mode), 2 fix commits (login redirect, pagination off-by-one), 1 security fix (XSS in comments), and 2 chore commits (update deps, fix CI).
evals.expected_output: CHANGELOG.md entry grouped by Added/Changed/Fixed/Security with commit references

assertions.name: version_header
assertions.description: ## [2.5.0] header with date

assertions.name: grouped_entries
assertions.description: Entries grouped under Added, Fixed, and Security sections

assertions.name: feat_as_added
assertions.description: feat commits listed under Added

assertions.name: fix_as_fixed
assertions.description: fix commits listed under Fixed

assertions.name: security_section
assertions.description: XSS fix listed under Security section

assertions.name: chore_excluded
assertions.description: chore commits not in user-facing changelog

evals.prompt: Our CHANGELOG.md hasn't been updated in 6 months. Generate entries for all releases between v1.0.0 and v1.5.0 from git history. We use conventional commits.
evals.expected_output: Multiple release entries generated from git log, each properly grouped

assertions.name: multiple_versions
assertions.description: Entries for each version between 1.0.0 and 1.5.0

assertions.name: git_log_based
assertions.description: Content derived from actual git commit messages

assertions.name: conventional_parsing
assertions.description: Commit type (feat/fix/etc) determines category

assertions.name: chronological
assertions.description: Newest version at top

assertions.name: keep_a_changelog
assertions.description: Follows Keep a Changelog format

evals.prompt: Generate a changelog entry for a breaking change release (v3.0.0). We removed the legacy /api/v1/ endpoints, renamed the User model to Account, and changed auth from cookies to JWT.
evals.expected_output: Major release changelog with BREAKING CHANGES section prominently featured

assertions.name: breaking_changes
assertions.description: BREAKING CHANGES section prominently listed

assertions.name: migration_notes
assertions.description: Notes on how to migrate from v2 to v3

assertions.name: removed_section
assertions.description: Removed endpoints listed under Removed

assertions.name: changed_section
assertions.description: Renamed model and auth change under Changed

assertions.name: major_version
assertions.description: Version header is 3.0.0

evals.prompt: Set up automated changelog generation in CI. When a release tag is pushed, generate the changelog from commits since the last tag and include it in the GitHub release notes.
evals.expected_output: CI workflow that auto-generates changelog on tag push and attaches to GitHub release

assertions.name: tag_trigger
assertions.description: Workflow triggers on tag push (v*)

assertions.name: changelog_generation
assertions.description: Generates changelog from commits since previous tag

assertions.name: github_release
assertions.description: Attaches changelog to GitHub release body

assertions.name: conventional_commits
assertions.description: Parses conventional commit format

assertions.name: automation
assertions.description: No manual changelog editing required

evals.prompt: Generate a user-facing release notes document (not developer changelog) for version 2.5.0. Translate technical commit messages into plain language that our non-technical users can understand.
evals.expected_output: User-friendly release notes with plain language descriptions and feature highlights

assertions.name: plain_language
assertions.description: Technical terms translated to user-friendly language

assertions.name: feature_highlights
assertions.description: New features described with user benefit, not implementation

assertions.name: bug_fixes_summarized
assertions.description: Bug fixes described as problems solved, not code changed

assertions.name: no_technical_jargon
assertions.description: No commit hashes, file paths, or code references

assertions.name: visual_appeal
assertions.description: Formatted for readability with headers and bullet points
